_comment: Real-shape Domain-Search wire fixture (HUN-20651 Phase 4). Captured to match app/app/views/api/domain_search/show.json.jbuilder EXACTLY, so shouldVerify is exercised against shapes that actually occur on the wire — NOT hand-authored payloads that omit `verification` or hoist `confidence` into the wrong place. Key invariants this fixture pins (verified against the jbuilder): (1) EVERY email entry carries a `verification: { date, status }` block — it is ALWAYS emitted; on a fresh email with no < 1-month EmailVerification row both leaves are null (the dominant case). (2) `confidence` is an ENTRY-level integer 0-100, never nested inside `verification`. (3) `accept_all` exists at BOTH the domain level (`data.accept_all`) and — rarely — per email entry. The default `data.accept_all` here is false; tests flip it via the domainAcceptAll param. (4) The non-null `verification.date` on the valid+high-confidence entry is recent because Rails strips any verification older than EmailVerification::VALIDITY_THRESHOLD = 1.month before rendering — so there is no stale-date arm to test. Mutate (deep-clone) this fixture to build the shouldVerify truth table; do not edit the leaves in place.
